annotations.anchor_quote: III. Impediments in the way of enlarging female mind with education
annotations.comment: Impediments section over-emphasizes with more sub-points than benefits; balance by distributing points evenly across sections.

annotations.anchor_quote: (2) In Pakistan 18% girls in age 12-16 get married.
annotations.comment: Statistic lacks source attribution; mention reliable source like UN or census data.

annotations.anchor_quote: Appaling sanitation conditions ingovernment schools
annotations.correction: Appalling sanitation conditions in government schools
annotations.comment: Correct spelling of 'Appalling' and separate 'ingovernment' into 'in government'.

annotations.anchor_quote: No sofe transport for female studends from for flung areas.
annotations.correction: No safe transport for female students from far-flung areas.
annotations.comment: Correct spellings: 'sofe' to 'safe', 'studends' to 'students', 'for flung' to 'far-flung'.

annotations.anchor_quote: Case in point: Patriarch head of the family SYED TAIN decides and provides.
annotations.comment: Sub-point is descriptive only; add analysis of how patriarchal decisions impede education access.

annotations.anchor_quote: II. How strengthening the female mind by enlarging it with education Godsends blind obedience: 1. Only educated female breaks free from the shackles of patriarchal mind set that demands blind obedience. 2. Educated female secures economic independence which protects from obeying mindlessly 3. Females strengthened by education lencew their rights and do not submit to injustice.
annotations.correction: Revise subpoints to include analytical depth with cause-effect links.
annotations.comment: Subpoints are merely descriptive and lack analytical depth; add cause-effect explanations to demonstrate how education ends blind obedience.

annotations.anchor_quote: I. Introduction It is an undeniable reality that by enlarging the female mind with education, there will be end to blind obedience, yet there are Various reasons which inhibit the process; consequently women across the globe suffer detrimental impacts. Hence, pragmatic policies and actions are needed to stream line women education
annotations.correction: Refine for clarity, grammar, and precise roadmap.
annotations.comment: Introduction addresses topic but lacks precise stance roadmap and has grammatical errors; explicitly outline main arguments, impediments, and measures.

annotations.anchor_quote: there will be end to blind obedience
annotations.correction: there will be an end to blind obedience
annotations.comment: Insert 'an' before 'end' to correct article usage and grammatical accuracy.

annotations.anchor_quote: Godsends blind obedience:
annotations.correction: ends blind obedience:
annotations.comment: Change 'Godsends' to 'ends' to fix misspelling and convey intended meaning accurately.

annotations.anchor_quote: stream line women education
annotations.correction: streamline women's education
annotations.comment: Merge 'stream line' to 'streamline' and add apostrophe to 'women' for correct spelling and possession.

annotations.anchor_quote: 5. Massive digital in present age of online learning. Case in Point: Global digital divide 68%, for women 51% (3) Pakistan female digital divide 24% whereas mode's is 48%
annotations.correction: Add analytical depth explaining cause-effect on blind obedience.
annotations.comment: Sub-point lists descriptive statistics without analytical depth; include how digital divide perpetuates blind obedience.

annotations.anchor_quote: 6. Formales lack of security outside the four walls of their home.
annotations.correction: 6. Females lack of security outside the four walls of their home.
annotations.comment: 'Formales' is misspelled; correct to 'Females'.

annotations.anchor_quote: Ensure "altidunal" change rather than more LawsD
annotations.correction: Ensure 'attitudinal' change rather than more laws.
annotations.comment: 'altidunal' is misspelled and 'LawsD' erroneous; correct to 'attitudinal change rather than more laws'.

annotations.anchor_quote: Global digital divide 68%, for women 51% (3) Pakistan female digital divide 24% whereas mode's is 48%
annotations.correction: Attribute statistics to reliable source.
annotations.comment: Digital divide statistics lack source; cite verifiable reference like UN or World Bank.

annotations.anchor_quote: these impediment in way of women education.
annotations.correction: these impediments in the way of women education.
annotations.comment: 'impediment' should be plural and preposition awkward; change to 'impediments in the way of women's education'.

annotations.anchor_quote: deprivation neves break.
annotations.correction: deprivation never breaks.

annotations.anchor_quote: what women cando, or can not;

annotations.anchor_quote: what women cando, or can not; what women can wear or wear not; what women can feel or feel not.

annotations.comment: Repetitive phrasing of restrictions reduces impact; vary sentence structure to enhance engagement.

annotations.anchor_quote: They do not let women think once for her own self and
annotations.correction: They do not let women think once for herself, breaking the cycle of blind obedience.
annotations.comment: Paragraph ends abruptly; add a concluding sentence to provide closure and smooth transition.

annotations.anchor_quote: It has, undeniably, wisely phrased that it needs education to break free females from blind obedience.
annotations.correction: It is undeniably true that education is needed to free females from blind obedience.
annotations.comment: Awkward opening fails to directly address key terms or provide explicit thesis and roadmap; rephrase to state stance clearly and preview main arguments, impediments, and measures.

annotations.anchor_quote: Any one who is educated, questions, thinks, rationalizes and only then make an action.
annotations.correction: Anyone who is educated questions, thinks, rationalizes, and only then makes an action.
annotations.comment: Subject-verb disagreement and punctuation errors; correct to singular 'makes', use 'Anyone', and add serial commas.

annotations.anchor_quote: They question and rationalize and only than decide on whether to comply or not.
annotations.correction: They question and rationalize before deciding whether to comply.
annotations.comment: Repeats earlier description of educated questioning; consolidate ideas to eliminate redundancy.

annotations.anchor_quote: By enlarging the femal mind is by virtue of educating them makes them better not only for their own good but also for the entire society.
annotations.correction: Enlarging the female mind through education makes them better not only for their own good but also for the entire society.
annotations.comment: Garbled run-on sentence and spelling error; restructure for clarity and fix 'femal' to 'female'.

annotations.anchor_quote: They make more than 50% of the world population
annotations.correction: Women make up approximately 50% of the world population (UN data).
annotations.comment: Unsourced factual claim; cite reliable source like UN and use precise figure.

annotations.anchor_quote: They suffer socially, politically and economically and generational cycles of women
annotations.correction: They suffer socially, politically, and economically, perpetuating generational cycles of women's deprivation.

annotations.anchor_quote: NV. Conclusion.
annotations.correction: Add subpoints under V. Conclusion for thesis summary and recommendations.
annotations.comment: Outline marks conclusion but lacks subpoints; develop structure to cover all major aspects including final synthesis.

annotations.anchor_quote: NV. Conclusion.
annotations.correction: V. Conclusion.
annotations.comment: Numbering error in 'NV. Conclusion.'; correct to 'V. Conclusion.' to follow Roman numeral sequence.

annotations.anchor_quote: 3. In Pakistan, ensure implementation of Article 25, and Article 38

annotations.anchor_quote: Hence, when the female is educated, she comes out of this vicious cycle of dependence and makes decisions for herself just as she likes.

annotations.anchor_quote: To conclude, it can be deduced that embellishing the female mind with education will protect her from blind compliance. She will rationalize before she does anything which will not only make her better, but world better. annotations.comment: Conclusion restates thesis and summarizes repetitively without new ideas or powerful closing; add compelling call to action for global change.
